title: Use FTS5 for full-text search
description: Index searchable text in an FTS5 virtual table kept in sync with the base table rather than scanning with LIKE.
body: Use when users search free text and LIKE '%term%' is too slow or unranked. Do not scan a large text column with leading-wildcard LIKE. Actions: create an FTS5 virtual table over the searchable columns, keep it in sync via triggers or the external-content/contentless option, and query with MATCH plus bm25() for ranking; pick a tokenizer (unicode61, porter, or trigram for substring). Validation signals include a search returning ranked results from the FTS index rather than a full scan, confirmed by query plan or timing. Risk boundary: an FTS table that drifts out of sync with the base table returns stale or missing results — verify the sync triggers cover insert, update, and delete.
